description_long: This object is a realistic model of a crustacean, resembling a small lobster or crayfish, with a segmented body featuring a robust cephalothorax and a tapered abdomen. The exoskeleton is mottled in shades of brown and tan, with subtle patterning that mimics natural coloration. Prominent claws (chelae) extend from the front, one noticeably larger than the other, and are flanked by several pairs of articulated walking legs. Two long, slender antennae project forward from the head, adding to the creature's sensory apparatus. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the cephalothorax, providing a wide field of vision. The overall texture appears hard and slightly rough, typical of a crustacean shell, with a matte to semi-gloss finish.
description: A detailed, brownish crustacean with segmented body, large claws, long antennae, and a hard, textured shell.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, both claws, and the long antennae extending forward.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the segmented abdomen, tail fan, and symmetrical arrangement of legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the claws, walking legs, and the curvature of the body.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the claws, antennae, and eyes on short stalks at the head.
